invariant: ADOPT Rule 2 — every AC in the stub carries the OPEN tag; hallucinating closed ACs is a defect (P1). Zero closed ACs is correct output.
owning_role: ADOPT (00-aprd/ADOPT.md) — Step 6 shapes aprd.frozen.md stub; Rule 2 mandates OPEN tag on every AC. A runner producing closed ACs has violated the discriminator.
seed: overlay defects/closed-acs/aprd.frozen.closed.md → .aprd/aprd.frozen.md (ACs without OPEN tag — 5 closed ACs authored by runner as if the requirements were known)
load_bearing_assertion: Golden aprd.frozen.md has every AC containing '[OPEN — client to fill at Checkpoint A]'. Defect aprd.frozen.md has ACs without that tag (closed, filled text). Value-parity fails: the OPEN-tag discriminator is absent in defect output.
expected_verdict: value-parity FAIL — closed ACs where OPEN tags required
separates_from_golden_by: presence of '[OPEN — client to fill at Checkpoint A]' tag in every AC (golden: all 5 present / defect: 0 present). The hallucinated closed ACs are a P1 violation — ADOPT's core discriminator.
e2e_validated: 2026-06-12 — clean-room step-runner ran prompts/00-aprd/ADOPT.md against the src/ bench (no ADP trees) → all 5 ACs carry OPEN tag (golden). Defect overlay: runner given closed-acs bench produces ACs lacking OPEN tag. Oracle separates on OPEN-tag presence per AC.
